Every great week starts the night before.

Before the panels, the demos, the packed rooftops, and the "we should definitely grab coffee" texts that never land—there's this. A room full of the people actually building Tech Week. The hosts. The sponsors. The operators behind Boston's best events. And their friends who somehow always know everyone.

This is the pre-game. Sunday evening. Downtown Boston. Cold pints, real conversations, and the kind of connections that make the rest of the week click. You'll meet the organizers you've been DMing, swap intel on the can't-miss events, and walk into the week already locked in.

No agenda. No slide decks. No lanyards. Just good people getting a head start.

PURCHASE YOUR TICKET HERE: 
https://www.eventbrite.com/e/aging-code-summit-tickets-1984636074925

--

Aging is becoming an engineering problem.

Multi-omic biomarkers. Longitudinal datasets at population scale. Foundation models trained on biological aging. Wearables and continuous monitoring feeding clinical-grade signal. Closed-loop intervention platforms. The same compute curve that rewrote software is now rewriting how we measure, model, and intervene on human aging.

Boston is the operating system for this shift. MIT, Harvard, the Broad, the hospital network, and the densest concentration of longevity biotechs on the planet, all within a four-mile radius. 

During Boston Tech Week, the Aging Code Summit pulls that stack into one room.

What you will see on stage:

- Principal investigators from MIT and Harvard shipping the science behind epigenetic clocks, senescence biology, neurodegeneration pipelines, and immune aging
- Founders and CSOs from the longevity companies turning that science into diagnostics, therapeutics, and active clinical trials
- The A…

properties.description:
Join First Star Ventures for a Boston Tech Week breakfast briefing designed specifically for limited partners evaluating deep tech exposure in their portfolios.

Software VC playbooks don't map cleanly onto synthetic biology, energy, or robotics but these are precisely the categories where the next wave of massive, durable, defensible outcomes is being built. Technical risk profiles, capital intensity, time-to-revenue, and exit dynamics all look different in deep tech, and LPs allocating here need frameworks to reflect that.

Over breakfast in Kendall Square (the densest square mile of deep tech innovation in the world), we'll cover:
* Capital intensity and dilution in deep tech startups: truths and myths
* Milestones that matter in early-stage, frontier technologies
* Current signal from the Boston/Cambridge ecosystem across chips, bio, and energy

Whether you're already allocating or still only evaluating, join us in Kendall Square to trade notes on what's working, what isn't, and what we're seeing across the deep tech ecosystem in Boston and beyond. Hosted by First Star Ventures.

This event is a part of #BOSTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring togeth…

properties.description:
The same AI revolution that puts a Fortune 500 company's capabilities in your pocket is also being used by authoritarian regimes to build pre-crime models, deploy real-time facial recognition, surveil ethnic minorities, and extract data from the phones of dissidents. The question is not whether AI will shape the future of freedom — it's who will shape AI.

Vibe Coding for Freedom is a full-day hackathon designed to answer that question practically, and put tools directly in the hands of the people who need them most.

What you'll walk away with:
You'll leave this day knowing how to use vibe coding tools to build the digital assets your organization actually needs — and understanding how AI can be both the threat and the solution when it comes to human freedom.

The agenda covers two tracks:
AI as a tool of repression — how regimes are using pre-crime modeling, facial recognition, ethnic minority tracking, and phone surveillance to build stronger police states, and what freedom advocates need to know.
AI as a tool of liberation — how open-source, privacy-protecting agents, organizational scaling tools, activist-focused super-prompts, and freedom tech infrastructure (including Bit…

properties.description:
AI has made it easier than ever to build.

Which means more people are building fast… and building the wrong things.

This session is for founders who don’t want to waste months building something no one actually needs.

In this interactive Problem Discovery Sprint, we’ll focus on what matters before any code is written: identifying real, high-impact problems worth solving.

You’ll work through a structured process to:

Break down real-world workflows and inefficiencies across industries like AI, healthcare, climate, and productivity
Identify where time, money, and effort are being wasted today
Separate “interesting ideas” from problems people will actually pay to solve

This is not a panel or a lecture. It’s a working session designed to help you think clearly and move forward.

By the end of the session, you’ll leave with:

A clearly defined problem statement
A specific target customer
A concrete next step to validate your idea

If you’ve been thinking about starting something but aren’t sure what’s actually worth building, this session will give you clarity.

Spots are limited to keep this session highly interactive.

This session offers a window into the future of collaboration as 50 creators build one experience within a collaborative system, reflecting how brands and agencies can work with AI in practice.‍

Black Math will pre-build the creative and technical foundation using ComfyUI. The system is designed to support contributions from people with different skill sets, tools, and levels of familiarity, all working within a shared framework.‍

Brand managers, Creative Directors, Tech Directors, AI Creators and first-time AI users alike will work in parallel, each contributing from their own perspective while shaping a single outcome together.‍

Organ Engineering-How Biofabrication is paving the way for Tissue Products - #BosTechWeek

From lab-grown tissue models used in drug discovery and disease research to the development of implantable tissue therapeutics, biofabrication is bridging a critical gap between experimentation and clinical application. The conversation will highlight how engineered tissues can better replicate human physiology, improve predictive testing, and open new pathways for regenerative medicine. Bringing together perspectives from science, engineering, and entrepreneurship, this event will provide a high-level look at where the field stands today and where it’s headed next. 

Join us for a fireside chat featuring Christopher S. Chen, a leading scientists in tissue engineering, mechanobiology, and tissue therapuetics. Dr. Chen is the William Fairfield Warren Distinguished Professor of Biomedical Engineering at Boston University, Founding Director of the Biological Design Center, and a Core Faculty member at the Wyss Institute for Biologically Inspired Engineering. His work has helped shape the modern field of engineered tissues and microphysiological systems, advancing how researchers model human b…

properties.description:
Raising a Series A is a fundamentally different process than raising a seed round. Expectations shift—from vision to execution, from storytelling to metrics, and from promise to proof. This panel is designed for seed-stage founders who want a clear, realistic view of what it takes to successfully raise a Series A in today’s market.

You’ll hear directly from industry professionals as to what Series A VCs actually look for, how founders derail otherwise strong raises, and how to prepare months in advance—not weeks.

Target Audience:
- Seed-stage founders planning to raise a Series A in the next 6–18 months
- Founders who have product-market fit signals but want clarity on readiness
- CEOs navigating early traction, hiring, and go-to-market scale
Founders who want to avoid common Series A fundraising mistakes

Topics of Discussion:
- What “Series A ready” really means in 2026 (metrics, momentum, narrative)?
- What are the key diligence questions Series A investors focus on and why, including pre-raise legal and financial considerations?
- How Series A expectations differ from seed across product, GTM, and team?
- How does a founder run a tight Series A process (timing, data room,…

properties.description:
Join Pickle Robot for an exclusive behind-the-scenes look at the company building Physical AI for supply chain automation. Founded by three MIT engineers, Pickle Robot is tackling the $100B global challenge of truck unloading with intelligent, collaborative robots that eliminate backbreaking manual labor.

This 90-minute session begins with an executive meet-and-greet at Pickle Robot HQ in Charlestown, where you’ll enjoy a light lunch, connect with the leadership team, and hear the founding story, company vision, and what’s next for Physical AI in supply chains.

You’ll then tour the office and innovation lab, stepping onto the warehouse floor for a live demo of Pickle’s robotic unloading system as AI-guided robots work at better-than-human speed. The experience wraps with an open Q&A and networking - perfect for making connections and getting your questions answered directly by the team building it.

properties.description:
Join us for a lunch conversation on Workflow Automation with Document Intelligence, bringing together leaders from wealth management, insurance, and pharmaceutical companies to discuss one of the biggest operational questions facing regulated industries:

How do you automate when your most important business data is still trapped in documents?

Across regulated enterprises, critical information still lives in PDFs, scans, forms, emails, policy documents, onboarding packets, claims files, clinical documents, compliance reports, and legacy records. These documents drive revenue, risk, service quality, compliance, and decision-making, but they are often handled through manual review, rekeying, reconciliation, and exception management.

As AI adoption accelerates, the bottleneck is becoming clear. The challenge is not just applying models. It is creating trusted, structured, auditable data from messy real-world documents so automation can actually work in production.

This lunch discussion will explore how forward-thinking teams are rethinking document-heavy operations, including:

How wealth management firms can accelerate onboarding, client servicing, compliance review, and adviso…

properties.description:
Connect Smarter at Tech Week!

Have you ever attended a large event with hundreds—or even thousands—of people, and felt unsure about who you should talk to?

Here's how it works:
Now – May 25, 6:00 PM → Sign-up is open. Create your Digital Twin by signing up and let it know what you're looking for at Tech Week.

May 26, 8:00 AM → Agents Meet begins. Your AI twin starts networking with other participants' agents—having conversations, finding common ground, and identifying your best matches while you go about your day.

May 26, 9:00 AM → Your twin wraps up and sends you a personalized email summarizing who you should meet and why, based on the conversations it had on your behalf.

This is a hands-on design workshop where we’ll explore how VIA leverages AI to simplify high-stakes data workflows without compromising protection. As NSA experts have told us, great security exists. The issue is when it's too cumbersome so people choose to ignore it.

The workshop: Learn actionable AI-design frameworks to make military-grade security feel human

We’ll also showcase LLM Compressor, an open source library for model quantization. As part of the workshop, we’ll deploy a quantized model together, then measure the impact on the AI metrics that matter most in production deployments. The session will conclude with an open community discussion and networking.

Who should attend: AI developers, ML engineers, platform engineers, data scientists, and anyone passionate about AI, data, and computer science who wants to get hands-on with modern, open source LLM deployment and optimization techniques.

What to bring: A fully charged laptop!

properties.description:
AI is a truly system design challenge that balances Compute, Storage and Network. With the inference market growing bigger than training, orchestration of these three infrastructure components is the key to Tokenomics driving the efficiency of every generated token.

This presentation explores the critical journey of scaling AI infrastructure, tracing the evolution from pre-silicon design and multi-die chiplet architectures all the way to fully synchronized data center clusters and edge deployments. Because AI workloads push devices well beyond baseline specifications, small physical margins can quickly compound into network timeouts. A single dropped packet can spike tail latency, inflate Job Completion Time (JCT), and degrade GPU utilization. 

You will also learn how AI is scaling in all dimensions, called Scale X (Scale In, Scale Up, Scale Out, Scale Across, and Scale Above), all at once. We present the Challenges in each dimension and how hyper scalers are solving these challenges.

This event is a part of #BosTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com
properties.title: Scaling AI Infrastructure - From Chiplets to Clusters By Keysight - #BosTechWeek

Whether you're monetizing your side hustle, scaling your business, or figuring out how to balance content creation with client work, this happy hour is for you.

properties.description:
Most founders don’t fail because they can’t build. They fail because they build…in a vacuum. This event fixes that.

We’re bringing together some of Boston’s earliest-stage startups for a night of real-time product testing, feedback, and beta user growth.

This is not a presentation or panel: it’s a startup showcase. 

Founders will be set up at dedicated stations around the room, where they’ll demo what they’ve built, ask for specific feedback, and invite attendees to sign up as early users for their MVP or Beta List.

You’ll walk around, try products, talk directly to founders, and (if you like it) become an early user.

Think: part startup showcase, part user research lab.

What to expect:
- 15 early-stage startups demoing live products + prototypes
- Founders actively asking for feedback (not just pitching)
- A room full of operators, builders, and curious users
- Opportunities to join beta lists and get early access

For founders: This is your chance to pressure test your product, refine your messaging, and hear what people actually think, before you scale it. 

For attendees: You’ll get a first look at what’s being built across Boston and a chance to shape it. Your feedbac…

properties.description:
Venture capital firms are more selective and demanding when it comes to financing these days, especially with regard to first financings by early stage startups. Join us for an informative panel where we’ll be discussing questions and topics such as:  

- When does it make sense to start approaching venture capital firms for investment? 
- ​What does it take for a startup to raise its first priced round these days?  
- ​What are early, growth, and late stage VCs investing in today? 
- What kind of business and financial metrics should a founder have in place before approaching venture firms for a priced round raise, whether Series Seed or Series A/B/C? 
- What do venture capitalists predict about the venture market for the remainder of 2026?

- Lindsey S. Mignano, Mignano Law Group

properties.description:
Many successful careers often don’t go straight.

Join us for a fireside with the founder of a scaled tech/media company, the founder of a fast-growing early stage startup, and a tech creator - each with a non-linear path across roles and industries, with bets taken that didn’t obviously connect at the time.

We’ll drill down into the decisions behind their paths: when to explore vs. double down, how to navigate moments with no clear next step, and how seemingly unrelated experiences compound over time.

Moderated by a physician-operator with a similarly diverse background across startups, consulting, and the tech ecosystem. 

This event is a part of #BOSTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

properties.description:
You've never been to a mixer like this one.

Forget the highlight reel. Forget the rehearsed pitch and the superhero LinkedIn post. For one night, we're trading all of that for the good, the bad, and the ugly.

Boston founders get on stage and share the pivoting moment — the night the deal fell through, the morning they realized the product was wrong, the quiet decision to start over.

7 minutes each. Vulnerable. A little embarrassing. Funnier than you'd expect. Every story is a lesson somebody paid full price for.

You sit back. You laugh. Maybe wince. Then catch yourself nodding. By the time the mixer kicks in, the networking dance is dead. You're just catching up with people you actually want to know.

🎯 Why You Should Attend
Hear the version founders don't save for the panel circuit. Get the lesson without paying full tuition. Feel the room loosen up — once one person tells the truth, everybody stops auditioning.

🙋 Who's Getting In This Room
Founders actively building. Not circling the idea. People at a pivoting moment, ready to make a real move. Boston founders plus visiting builders in town for Tech Week.

📋 Run of Show
30 min · Check-in — Arrive, grab a drink
45 min · Op…

properties.description:
Let's kick-off #BOSTechWeek by setting the table! Join us for dinner at the best Brazilian Steakhouse in Boston along with other Founders and CTOs navigating how AI is reshaping the way teams are built and scaled.

​Honest conversations about talent strategy, what's actually working, where things break down, who you're really hiring for in 2026, rarely happen in public. This is a space for that.

​We'll sit down with a handful of peers already rethinking their engineering org: how they're balancing speed with quality, where nearshore fits (or doesn't), and how AI is changing expectations on both sides of the hire.

​No slides. No pitches. Just a candid dinner with people working through the same questions you are. Seats are limited and curated.

​​This is invite-only. Request access by applying via the RSVP form.

properties.description:
Some companies fail because of market changes. Others fail because they run out of money or can’t execute fast enough. Now more than ever, companies break before they even realize what’s happening.

They hire the wrong people, avoid hard conversations, trust the wrong signals, and build systems that quietly compound bad decisions. Now AI is accelerating all of it.

As part of Boston Tech Week, Dune IRL is partnering with Merraine Group and Galileo to host a candid conversation about scaling the right teams, both human and agentic.

Joining the conversation is Mike Lazerow, co-founder of Buddy Media (acquired by Salesforce for $745m) and an active investor who has built and scaled teams through real inflection points.

This won’t be another boilerplate “future of AI” panel. We’ll talk about bad hires, fragile systems, leadership mistakes, AI masking dysfunction, and signals most people ignore until it’s too late.

Expect sharp perspectives, honest stories, and practical insights from people who’ve made these decisions with real stakes. Followed by a curated networking session with founders and operators actively building and hiring.

6:00pm - 6:30pm Welcome
6:30pm - 7:15pm Panel…

properties.description:
Every CTO and CIO knows that the hardest part of transformation isn't finding the right technology — it's making the whole system move together. Visibility breaks down at the seams between tools. 

Governance erodes where ownership is unclear. And trust in your stack comes not from any single vendor, but from how everything holds under pressure.
Insight helps technology leaders navigate exactly that complexity — bringing visibility, governance, and integration discipline to environments that have outgrown their original design. 

As a leading Solutions Integrator with 35+ years of expertise and 30,000+ global clients, we design, build, and manage environments that scale without accumulating risk.

Exclaimer solves a specific, stubborn digital brand governance and compliance problem: email signatures. Every message leaving your organization carries brand, legal, and compliance weight — and most orgs have zero control over it. Together, we represent something CTOs and CIOs rarely get from their vendors: a complete solution to a real enterprise problem.

No pitch. No deck. Just a small group of senior technology leaders, a competitive evening on the F1 simulators, and honest conver…

properties.description:
"Taste" is everywhere right now — founder threads, investor memos, job descriptions asking for it like it's a software skill. But nobody agrees on what it actually means, or whether you can learn it. Is it design intuition? Pattern recognition from years of building? A million AI-generated logos, landing pages, and product concepts hit the internet today, and most of them are mid. The difference between what ships and what sticks is someone in the room who can tell the difference.

Wix Ventures and C House are hosting an intimate dinner at C House in Cambridge to get into it. Builders and creatives, breaking down design, curation, product instinct, and the human skills that can't be prompted. Dinner is on us (and yes, it's going to be a tasty one!).

---
This event is part of #BOSTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.
properties.title: Taste: The Human Skill AI Hasn't Automate (Yet...)

properties.description:
The best conversations happen at 7:30am with your running shoes on. 

Start your morning with a 5k group run along the Charles River, alongside people building interesting things with AI. Hosted by Formation Bio, all paces welcome. 

NOTE: Photo ID is required to enter the building. 

We'll finish at our Back Bay office for breakfast, coffee, and a chance to connect before the rest of Tech Week has even poured their first cup. Meet the engineers and scientists building AI for drug development, and find out about the unique problems they get to solve everyday. Come if you're an engineer, scientist, or builder, grad student, work in AI or healthcare, or are just curious about it. Formation's CBO will be there to welcome and host. 

Not a runner? Join us at 8:15 for coffee and breakfast. You’ll leave energized, connected, and with Formation Bio swag.

We’ll run rain or shine, but in the event of severe weather, we'll go straight to breakfast at the office. You'll get a heads-up the morning of.

See you out there!

properties.description:
As AI surges forward and new products enter the market every day, the real challenge is no longer innovation; it is impact. What actually makes an AI product stick inside the enterprise, scale, and deliver lasting value?

Join Glasswing Ventures, Goodwin, HSBC, and Techstars for Defensible AI, a conversation focused on how enterprise leaders are evaluating AI today and what truly separates durable solutions from passing momentum.

We will explore how decisions are being made around adoption, what signals matter most from early traction to enterprise scale, and how the next generation of innovators and investors are approaching defensibility with intention.

properties.description:
Join the Wistia team in Boston for an exclusive half-day session built for marketing teams who need to create more (and better) video without slowing down. We’ll start with key insights from our latest 2026 State of Video Report, then get into the tactics, how teams are producing high-impact content and where AI is actually making a difference (not just the hype).

You’ll walk away with practical frameworks, a clearer sense of what to prioritize, and ideas you can put to work right away.

Between sessions, explore demo stations, connect with other marketing leaders, and ask the questions you can’t Google. Nothing’s recorded, so you’ll get the real, unfiltered version.

properties.description:
Where Does the Talent Go?
Making Sense of Tech Careers in the AI Shakeup

Every week there's a new LinkedIn post from someone who says they cut their team from 50 to 2 using AI. The comments go wild. Half the replies are "this is the future" and the other half are "this is short sighted". Nobody's actually unpacking what's true, what's noise, and what it means for the people building careers in tech right now.

That's what this conversation is about.

We're bringing together operators, founders, and investors who are close enough to the shift to see what's actually happening. Not the PR version and certainly not the engagement-bait on social version. Who's getting hired (and why), who's getting cut, what teams actually look like on the other side, and how experienced tech professionals can prep themselves to win in this new world.

We're bringing together a small, sharp panel followed by real conversation.

Meet the panel: 
Mike Gutner, Chief People Officer at Toast, Inc.
Sharon Pollard, VP of People & Culture at CloudZero
Kelley Morse, Chief People Officer at Bullhorn
Peter Zotto, CEO & Founder at Trove 

Come with questions you'd ask a friend in the industry over coffee, not t…

Unlike most conversations about AI in higher education, this session moves beyond theory and predictions into active design. Hosted by the Future of Higher Ed (FOHE) Boston in partnership with IDEO, the event brings together students, faculty, institutional leaders, and EdTech founders for a hands-on exploration of how AI is actually shaping the student experience and how students believe it should be.

What makes this session distinct is its starting point: students. The program opens with an undergraduate student-only panel that surfaces real tensions, unmet needs, and opportunities emerging on campus today. From there, participants shift into mixed-role design teams to co-create practical, student-centered solutions for an AI-enabled future of learning.

This is not a passive conversation. It is a working session grounded in real challenges, designed to generate actionable ideas and new ways of thinking about implementation, access, and impact.
Participants will leave not just with perspective, but with concrete insights and at least one idea they can take back and apply within their own institution or organization.

AI is already making decisions that impact customers, operations, and financial outcomes, yet most organizations cannot explain how those decisions are made, what context informed them, or who is accountable for them.

This panel brings together leaders at the forefront of AI governance to explore a critical shift: governance is no longer about policy documents. It’s about traceability, accountability, and the ability to defend decisions in real time. As regulatory pressure increases and AI adoption accelerates, organizations that cannot inspect and justify their AI-driven decisions will face real risk.

Join senior leaders, builders, and operators for a candid discussion on what AI governance actually looks like in practice — from enterprise implementation to board-level accountability. If you're responsible for deploying, overseeing, or investing in AI, this conversation will challenge how you think about control, risk, and execution.

properties.description:
Tired of hearing about OpenClaw agents but still haven’t had time to actually set one up?

Now you have an excuse.

During this hands-on Boston Tech Week workshop, we’ll help you deploy an AI agent on the cloud using OpenClaw, ZeroClaw, Hermes, or your own agent. No infrastructure experience required.

You’ll learn how to:

Set up an AI agent in the cloud

Choose between OpenClaw, ZeroClaw, Hermes, or build your own agent

Configure the agent for your specific use case

Use Maritime’s library of configurable templates

Customize an existing template instead of starting from scratch

Save your setup as a shareable config

Share your agent template with friends, teammates, or the broader community

Understand basic security and deployment best practices

By the end, you’ll have a live agent running in the cloud and a better understanding of how to make agents useful for real workflows.

No tech experience required. Just bring your laptop.

properties.description:
Docling is an open-source document processing framework developed by IBM Research that converts unstructured documents (like PDFs, DOCX, PPTX, and images) into clean, structured, machine-readable data. It is designed to bridge the gap between messy, real-world business documents and AI.

In this build session, we'll be walking through the features of Docling.  By the end, you will be able to use Docling to:
- Convert complex documents into various formats suitable for AI
- Understand the various methods of Chunking and how to use them
- Create a transparent and verifiable multimodal RAG application
- Navigate a parsed document's section tree with an LLM, without chunking or embeddings
- Deploy Docling as a scalable REST API service
- Compare token costs for PDF Q&A with and without Docling preprocessing
- Integrate Docling as a tool for AI agents using the Model Context Protocol (MCP)

Bring a laptop. The session is open to engineers, AI builders, and PMs who are interested in leveraging the latest open source tools for software development.
___________

This is the second in a series of four "Build-n-Brew" sessions to be held in the afternoon of each day during Boston Tech Week…
properties.title: MIT-IBM / The Open Accelerator - Build-n-Brew Session 2 -- Docling - #BOSTechWeek

properties.description:
AI is transforming GTM faster than any shift in the past decade. 

Outreach is automated. Content is generated instantly. Entire workflows are being rebuilt with AI at the core.

But one thing hasn’t changed: humans still make buying decisions.

The companies pulling ahead aren’t replacing sales teams. They’re redefining them. They’re building AI-enabled revenue engines while doubling down on the human skills that actually close deals: judgment, trust, positioning, and execution.

In this conversation, moderated by JR Butler, CEO of Shift Group, we're bringing together both old school and new school sales leaders:

- Chris Ward, CRO, Jellyfish
- Dino DiMarino, CRO, Tenable 
- John Hanlon, Experienced Sales Leader (Presidio, EMC)
- Brooke Duffy, Fractional CRO
- Brett Johnson, COO, Gauntlet AI 

Together, they've led sales organizations across every era of GTM – from the pre-SaaS playbook to the AI-native stack.

Join us to learn what’s changing, what still works, and how the best revenue teams are combining proven sales instincts with AI to scale faster.

properties.description:
AI isn't coming for marketing. It's already here, and it's rewriting the rules faster than most organizations can keep up.

The organizations getting ahead aren't just experimenting with AI tools. They're redesigning how marketing works: automating workflows to make teams more efficient, using AI to personalize at scale, and rebuilding revenue operations from the ground up. Everyone in the org is now a content producer, and marketing is being asked to lead the structure around that. Meanwhile, your CEO and board have made AI adoption a mandate, not a suggestion.

Demand Spring and Brandlight are bringing together a small group of senior marketing leaders in Boston for an evening of real conversation. You'll hear briefly from our AI and revenue marketing experts, then spend the rest of the night in the kind of candid, peer-to-peer conversations that are hard to find anywhere else. Brandlight will also share how leading brands are staying visible as buyers shift to AI tools first in their research process.

Come ready to share, not just listen. Space is limited.

Drinks, good food, and the best views in Boston included.

properties.description:
The org chart is breaking. AI agents are replacing early hires, and most founders are scaling without a playbook for what their team should actually look like.

This is a panel + hands-on workshop for pre-seed to Series B founders and you leave with a redrawn org map and a 30-day action list.

We'll cover:

1) Why the old org chart fails at 15 people (and how to fix it)
2) Real org structure cases by stage: pre-seed through Series B
3) Dynamic Teaming, a framework for building a team that evolves as your company does
4) How to use fractional generalists, AI agents, and Northeastern co-op talent to go further without burning cash

Hosted by Britt Gage, founder of of All Trades, and Nancy Tarr, Senior Co-op Faculty at Northeastern whose co-op program places students who work as true team members, not interns.

For founders who want to stop guessing at org design and actually build for what's next.

Please bring your computer to be able to participate!

properties.description:
The best exits don't happen by accident. Neither do the best acquisitions.

Built Different brings together a panel of experienced operators, investors, and dealmakers who have lived every stage of the M&A journey — from building a company with intention, to navigating an acquisition, to executing a successful exit. This is the conversation that happens after the deal closes, brought to the room before you need it.
We'll cover the full arc: how to grow a company that's positioned for success, what it actually takes to buy or sell, and how to set yourself up to win at every stage of the process. 

Then stick around for happy hour and connect with the dealmakers, operators, and investors in the room and keep the conversation going over drinks.

This event is for: Leaders building a company with an eye toward what's next · Executives exploring acquisitions as a growth strategy · Operators and investors who want an honest look at what M&A really looks like · Anyone who has been through a deal and wants to do the next one better

properties.description:
As companies start to scale, founders often find themselves facing a new question: beyond building my company, how can I use this journey to create broader impact in the world?

Join Founders Pledge at Boston’s eye-popping Museum of Ice Cream to celebrate the inaugural Boston Tech Week with a candid panel, "Impact by Design: Embedding Purpose into the Founder Journey," featuring founders who’ve thought deeply about how to integrate purpose, philanthropy and long-term impact throughout the process of building, not just at the point of exit.

We’ll explore how building companies shaped how they think about their larger purpose, what it means to move beyond the traditional idea of “giving back”, and how founders can approach philanthropy with the same ambition, rigor and intentionality they bring to their founder role. 

Together we’ll create an intimate space to connect with a community of founders committed to embedding purpose into success from day one and cool off with frozen treats (dairy ice cream and dairy-free options will be available!).

properties.description:
Making the Call: When to Build AI Solutions & How to Drive Adoption

We are still in the early innings of the AI era. Across consumer and B2B products, the expectations to leverage AI are massive, but is it always the right fit?

A core responsibility of a Product Manager is identifying the right solution based on user feedback and problem-fit, rather than simply chase the "shiny new" AI idea.

Our panel of experts will share real-world examples of:

* The decisions behind their AI roadmaps: why they moved forward (or didn't).
* How they successfully gained user adoption and generated measurable value.
* Lessons learned - what didn't work and what I would do differently next time.
* And more!

Speakers:

* Craig Daniel, SVP & GM at Toast
* Shailja Dedakia, Head of Product at Capsule
* Krishna Kannan, Head of Product at Jellyfish
* Jamie Conroy, Senior Principal Product Manager at CMT
* Harriet Brown, Chief Product Officer at Mobi

Agenda:

5:30pm - Registration & Networking
6:30pm - Panel
7:30pm - More Networking
8:00pm - Wrap Up

Thank you to CMT for hosting this event!

This event is a part of #BOSTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech…
properties.title: Making the Call: When to Build AI Solutions & How to Drive Adoption - #BOSTechWeek

properties.description:
The leaders building Boston's hardest tech aren't meeting at conferences—they're meeting over dinner. A curated table of CTOs, VPEs, and CPTOs from companies like Whoop, CloudZero, and Markforged, wrestling with the question nobody's fully answered yet: where does product end and engineering begin when AI rewrites the rules? 

AI isn't just changing what your teams build—it's dismantling the walls between *who* builds and *who* decides what gets built. Product and engineering are bleeding into each other faster than anyone predicted, and the leaders who figure out the new shape first will pull ahead of everyone else.

We'll dig in with thoughtful discussion: Are integrated builder teams the future or a fantasy? How are you reworking your SDLC? What org design changes are you making right now—what are you theorizing about?

This is a seated dinner at Committee in the Seaport—an intentional room of leaders from ambitious Boston tech companies. No panels. No keynotes. Just sharp people and honest conversation.

Presented by VPE Forum, a community of senior engineering leaders across Boston startups, growth-stage, and scaled companies

properties.description:
Boston does not get enough credit.

When people talk about startup cities, it is always New York and San Francisco. Boston gets dismissed as a college town with great hospitals. That is lazy.

Boston is quietly producing some of the most fundable and defensible startups in the country. The city has world-class research institutions, deep biotech and SaaS talent, and a cost structure that still gives founders a better shot at keeping equity instead of lighting it on fire.

The ecosystem is tighter. The networks are more accessible. The builders care more about substance than hype.

🎯 Why You Should Attend
-Hear why some founders chose Boston on purpose and why others moved there to build.
-Get a clearer read on what the city actually does well for startup operators.
-Understand where Boston gives founders an edge, and where it still falls short.
-Meet builders who care more about real company building than looking important online.

🙋 Who's Getting In This Room
-Boston founders and startup operators
-SaaS, biotech, AI, and deep tech builders
-Investors and ecosystem leaders active in the city
-Founders deciding where to build their next chapter
-People who want the good, the bad,…

Most founders build their product first and find their user second. Fewer can clearly articulate who it’s truly for. Even fewer have intentionally designed how that user will recognize, trust, and engage with it.

This session is about closing the gap, before that great idea becomes just a good idea.

Using live song overlays, you’ll experience what user alignment feels like, before we translate it into the diagnostic work.

This working session combines a short musical activation with a live diagnostic to help you identify your target user types, begin to explore what shapes their decisions, and consider what lands in their world, not just yours. Together we’ll explore why some ideas scale while others stall…and the difference between attention and alignment.

•  Founders and builders
•  Operators and strategists

properties.description:
Most of us are in the same loop. You have an idea. You workshop it with ChatGPT, read another newsletter, tell three friends who don't quite get it. The idea stays in your head. Or worse — it never gets the kind of pushback that would have made it sharper.
Research on creative work is clear: people who articulate their idea to like-minded peers begin to understand it deeper. Quality feedback isn't validation — it's the thing that changes how you see your own work.
We mix the room on purpose. Different industries see the same process from different angles — that's where value compounds. We ask for your LinkedIn in registration so we can group you with the right people, not random tables.
How the evening runs (3 stages)
Stage 1 — Themed Networking
You vote on the night's themes through the registration form (Fundraising, HR, GTM, Product, Pricing, Hiring — whichever wins the room). We group you by background. Each group's representative shares the conversation's main takeaway on stage. Featured contributors get a Featured Founder spotlight on ascentvalley.com — that's how the conversation scales across the ecosystem.
Stage 2 — Pitches
5 curated startups present their idea for live…

properties.description:
What if the hardest test for physical intelligence isn't a surgical suite — but performative art?

Art at the Edge of Physical AI is a fireside exploring what physical AI can truly do through the lenses of art: the dexterity, improvisation, and hardware it would take for a robot to co-create a room-scale botanical sculpture live on stage — and how those same frontier capabilities map directly to what's being built for industries. 

About the host: Peter is a physical AI investor at Climactic and an artist creating sculptures that are alive. His large-scale botanical works have been shown at the San Francisco Asian Art Museum, City Hall, and Fort Mason. He's developing a 2027 stage performance with robots; this fireside is where the thinking happens out loud.

This event is a part of #BostonTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

properties.description:
You've been sprinting. Three events a night. Back-to-back intros. Your voice is half gone and your phone's at 9%.

Breathe.

Thursday morning, the people of Boston Tech Week are pressing pause. Event hosts, sponsors, operators, founders—we're all meeting up in Kendall Square for coffee and a real conversation before diving back into the chaos.

This isn't a networking event. There's no agenda, no pitch timer, no name tags. It's the mid-week exhale you didn't know you needed—surrounded by the exact people who've been building the week's most memorable moments.

This time, we've got a loose thread to pull on together: AI reliability and trust. 2026 is shaping up to be the year agents actually go into production—in front of customers, inside teams, making real decisions. So bring your take. Are we there yet? What's still broken? What would it take for you to trust an agent with something that matters? Or just come for the vibes.

Compare notes. Trade recommendations. Or just sit with your cortado and exist for a second.

If you're hosting, sponsoring, speaking, or just deeply embedded in the #BOSTechWeek action, this room is your room.

📍 Kendall Square

🗓️ Thursday, May 28th · 7–9…

No presentations. No sponsors. Just great coffee, thoughtful conversation, and the chance to connect with people who are quite interesting.

properties.description:
Most people know they are leaving performance on the table.

In a world that rewards sustained output, the edge increasingly comes from how well you recover, focus, and adapt over time. Sleep, recovery, and resilience are not just wellness priorities. They are foundational inputs into both peak performance and long-term health.

The science has been clear for years. Now, technology and AI are accelerating how quickly data becomes insight, and how quickly insight turns into action.
RISE is a breakfast gathering for founders, operators, and investors who want to go deeper on what high performance actually requires, not just in moments, but over time.

Over breakfast, we will explore the inputs that compound quietly: starting with sleep, moving into recovery and inflammation, and expanding into the tools making performance and longevity more measurable and actionable than ever.

Join us for a thoughtful conversation, practical insights, and a chance to connect with others building at the intersection of performance, health, and technology.

Speakers to date:
N.B. Patil, Co-founder & CEO, Ozlo
Vijay Vaswani, Co-founder & CEO, Omniscope

This event is a part of #BOSTechWeek—a week of…

properties.description:
A hackathon competition pushing the boundaries of autonomous scientific research.

Teams will build systems of specialized AI agents that hypothesize, experiment, and discover with minimal human intervention. Each team will create two agents: a Run Agent that generates hypotheses, runs experiments, and produces research papers, and a Review Agent that critiques papers from other teams’ agents.

All agents will be released into a live shared ecosystem alongside every other team’s agents, where they will write, review, and iterate autonomously. Teams will compete to build the most capable autonomous research system, with final judging based on the quality, creativity, rigor, and scientific promise of their agents’ work.

We recommend teams of 4–5 people. Participants may come with a team or form one at the event.

This event is for researchers and engineers working across agentic AI, scientific reasoning, multi-agent systems, and computational biology. If you’re building at the frontier of AI-driven science, this is the room to be in.

Bring a laptop; we’ll provide the challenge, judging criteria, and technical setup at the start of the event.

This event is part of #BOSTechWeek —…

AI has permeated government systems, from Medicaid operations, national defense, and local infrastructure and public safety, but the path from pilot to production is still uneven, opaque, and often misunderstood.

This panel brings together founders, operators, and investors working directly at the intersection of AI and the public sector to unpack what’s happening on the ground. 

The conversation will focus on how AI is being adopted across different levels of government; how procurement and decision-making processes are evolving; and what it really takes for companies to sell, deploy, and scale within these systems.

properties.description:
AI agents are racing into HR, finance, sales, and ops, but most “agent platforms” have one fatal flaw: once an agent is live, almost no one can explain or control what it can actually touch. This workshop shows how Karini’s agentic AI foundation builds a real zero trust perimeter around agents so they can safely operate across the entire enterprise without blowing up security, compliance, or reputational risk.

You will see live, no‑BS examples of multi team agent meshes, HR and payroll automations, and CRM sales copilots that inherit the user’s identity, enforce least privilege, and log every decision. We break down inbound control (who can invoke which agents, with which roles) and outbound control (what those agents can see and do) using identity propagation, role based access to tools and data, per user OAuth scopes, and human checkpoints where it matters.

Investors will see why secure, governable agent meshes are the next defensible layer in the AI stack, not just another feature. Business leaders will walk out with a mental model and a checklist they can take to their CISO and board. Builders will leave with patterns they can implement now, and a clear view of why Karini…

properties.description:
The biggest barriers to AI in manufacturing aren’t technical—they’re human.

In this fireside chat, Ben Armstrong (Executive Director, MIT Industrial Performance Center), Liz Asai (Global Head of AI & Connectivity at Hologic), Pete Gowac (Sr. Director of U.S. Operations at Insulet), Alvin Clark (Sr. AI Engineer at NVIDIA), and Daniel Cummins (Fellow/VP, ISG Edge Solutions Platforms at Dell Technologies) explore the often-overlooked layer of AI adoption: trust, workforce dynamics, and the realities of implementing change inside complex manufacturing environments.

From frontline skepticism to leadership misalignment, we’ll unpack why even well-built systems fail to gain traction—and what successful teams are doing differently to bring their people along.

This is a discussion for those responsible for making AI real: plant leaders, operations executives, and investors looking to understand where value is actually created—or lost.

properties.description:
Can your AI agent find your data, understand what it means, and access only what it's allowed to — without anyone's help?

In almost every conversation we have with technology leaders, the same problem surfaces. The AI investment has been made. The data structure underneath was never ready for it. Siloed sources, no discoverability, unstable access controls, formats that work for humans but break models.

This session is for CDOs, CTOs, VPs of Data and Engineering, and Heads of AI who are closing that gap inside their own organizations.
We invited technology leaders and executives from ZoomInfo and other Boston-area companies to work through this together.

The Talk — What "AI-Ready Data" Actually Means and How Far You Really Are
A 10-layer readiness model built from work across dozens of enterprise data environments. Open standards, battle-tested patterns — not vendor frameworks.
What you walk away with:

A concrete score of where your organization stands today
Specific implementation steps for each layer
A clear path forward you can put in front of leadership

No pitches. Just the diagnostic.

properties.description:
Most technology pilots start with a handshake and end in a drawer. How do you make sure yours actually scales?

Join the Boston Smaht chapter of Building Intelligence Group and the US PropTech Council for a brutally honest panel on what it really takes to get a building technology pilot off the ground — and across the finish line.

We're bringing together owners, vendors, and advisors to have the conversation the industry usually avoids:

• Why do owners insist on pilots — and is it really about the technology?
• What does a pilot actually cost a vendor that never shows up on any invoice?
• Should pilots be paid — and what does your answer say about how serious you are?
• Who's really responsible when a pilot succeeds but the rollout fails?
• How do you define success before you start, not after?

This isn't a product showcase. It's a room full of people who've been on both sides of the table — and are willing to say what they actually think.

Come for the insights. Stay for the best networking in Boston's PropTech scene.

This event is a part of #BosTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

properties.description:
Most agents die in staging. Here's how to build ones that don't w/ IBM Bob and WXO

The future of software is going to be built with AI agents, but most agents never make it to production today. The ones that do often take months to harden, and even then, it's unclear whether they are delivering the value they were built for, or quietly burning tokens.

In this hands-on session, we'll bring together two halves of the agent lifecycle. We will use IBM Bob to build an agent from natural language. Then we will deploy it to watsonx Orchestrate, and take you through the full agent-ops cycle -- observability, evaluation and optimization -- to make your agent production ready.

Bring a laptop. The session is open to engineers, AI builders, and PMs who are interested in building agents that do more than demo well.
________

properties.description:
AI for protein design just won the Nobel Prize. Generative models are designing entire genomes from scratch. Researchers are using AI to predict drug responses, detect disease from a chest X-ray before symptoms appear, and design novel biomolecules that evolution never imagined. We've entered a moment where biology is becoming as designable, testable, and iterable as software.

Enabling this are entirely new data layers emerging around the human body. A decade ago, continuously tracking your heart rate was a novelty. Now we have platforms decoding our physiology in real time, generating the kind of longitudinal, high-resolution biological data that AI models are hungry for. The feedback loop between biological measurement and biological intervention is tightening. "Healthcare" as we think of it today may soon be something closer to human engineering. This convergence is creating a new kind of stack where the compute platform isn't a chip. It's you.

​Join John Capodilupo (co-founder/CPO of Throne), Conor Loy (co-founder/CEO of Romix Bio), and Daniel Delubac (co-founder/CEO of iOrganBio) for a conversation about building at this intersection.

​John Capodilupo dropped out of Harv…

properties.description:
We’re thrilled to unite the Elastic and Chainguard communities for an evening of learning and connection.

Join us to learn something new and meet others who are passionate about search, observability, and security.

Thursday, May 28th
4 Longfellow Pl. Boston, MA 02114

Agenda:

5:30 pm: Doors open; say hi and eat some food
6:00 pm: DriftlessAF: An open source agentic reconciliation framework proven at scale, by Maxime Gréau, Principal Software Engineer at Chainguard
6:30 pm: Q&A with Maxime
6:40 pm: The Agent That Retrieves Best, Wins, by AJ Meyers, Principal Solutions Architect at Elastic
7:10 pm: Q&A
7:20 - 7:30 pm: Networking and event wrap-up

Abstracts:

DriftlessAF: An open source agentic reconciliation framework proven at scale

At Chainguard, we build and maintain over 2,000 unique containers, hundreds of thousands of package versions, and hundreds of CVE patch backports. Our old event-driven architecture couldn't keep up: brittle queues, duplicate failures, lost work items. We had to start over.

The answer was DriftlessAF, our open source agentic reconciliation framework. A fleet of AI agents continuously watches for drift between the real world and the desired state,…

properties.description:
Architecting Startups is a Boston Tech Week conversation at Harvard GSD on architects building in tech, product, and venture.

Architecture trains people to think in systems, navigate ambiguity, understand human needs, and turn ideas into reality. Those are startup skills.

Join us for a live conversation with founders and leaders who started in architecture and went on to build companies, products, and careers in technology. We’ll talk about nontraditional paths into startups, why architectural thinking is such a powerful advantage in company building, and how design-led leaders operate across product, innovation, and venture.

You’ll leave with a clearer sense of how architectural training can translate into startup and tech careers, what skills carry over most powerfully, and how builders from design backgrounds can carve their own path beyond traditional practice.

For architects, designers, students, founders, operators, and anyone interested in nontraditional paths into tech.

Hosted by Build Momentum and moderated by Michael Meo, Harvard GSD alum and founder of Build Momentum.

Location shared upon approval.

properties.description:
Tech Week is great, but how many panels on AI regulation can you attend? It’s time to get your hands dirty.

Welcome to the Vibe Debugging Party, an interactive, competitive-ish, "escape room" style event for engineers, SREs, developers, and anybody who enjoys solving puzzles under mild (and friendly!) pressure.

We’ve set up a complex, modern microservices application running on Kubernetes, with a top-tier observability stack - OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Jaeger, etc. pre-configured.

Your challenge: We are going to intentionally, and secretly, break it.

You’ll work in small teams (or solo!) to triage the incident, trace the issue through the logs and traces, perform a Root Cause Analysis (RCA), and propose a fix. No actual coding is required, but you'll need all your observability and debugging intuition.

Is this for me?
If you’ve ever found a bug in a distributed system, understood what a flame graph is, or enjoy the phrase "it’s always DNS," then yes. We will have scenarios for different experience levels.

What to expect:
- Infrastructure-as-a-Gift: A fully-provisioned, broken system for you to explore.
- Chaos Engineering: Unknown, injected failure scenarios.
- RCA Glory: Be…

Hey there! 👋
The world is awash with cookie-cutter happy hours in a too-loud bar. This is a backyard sauna hangout at Chris Tolles' house in West Cambridge during Boston Tech Week. Come hang!

What? 🧖
Join us for a chill evening in the backyard with sauna for those inclined to partake. No pitches, no sponsors, just genuine conversation and good vibes with climate homies. 

The sauna holds four people, six if you are friendly, and will be available all evening. If you plan to sauna (AND YOU SHOULD), it’s easiest if you wear your bathing suit to the event, but there is a private bathroom inside the house if you need. 

Who? 👪
You! Climate founders, investors, operators, and fans. The yard is only so big, so attendance will be capped and climate people prioritized. 

When? 🗓️
We'll get started at 7PM on Thursday, May 28 and go until ~10PM (or as long as sauna party vibes persist).

Where? 🗺️
Chris lives in West Cambridge near Fresh Pond. It's an easy Uber or you can take the bus direct from Harvard Sq. The address will be shared once you're approved to attend. There's free street parking which technically requires a parking pass but it's rarely enforced.

Join us for a breakout session over coffee where Setapp by MacPaw, our partners from ByDesign, and iOS developer Jesse Waites will explore what it takes to turn an app idea into a sustainable product.

The event will feature a discussion on different parts of the app development lifecycle, including diverse perspectives of app growth, AI-assisted app development, and opportunities for indie devs. Speakers include:
- Pavlo Haidamak, Product Manager at Setapp – on developer relations, platform strategy, and app monetization
- Arthur Emma and Chirali Jain, founders of ByDesign – on building and scaling an app as a growing product team and Setapp partner 
- Jesse Waites, Founder of JTPCK.com – on the realities of indie iOS development and navigating the app landscape

Attendees can have 1:1 talks with each of our speakers after the discussion concludes, or simply stick around, work from the space, and connect with others over coffee and breakfast.

🥐 Coffee + light breakfast 
💬 Small group convos + 1:1 time 
🚀 Real talk on building, scaling, and monetizing apps…

properties.description:
Reign Dragon Lab is hosting a small research roundtable on the emerging governance problem of the AI workforce.

As AI agents move from tools into organizational actors, they will increasingly coordinate, delegate, compete, represent users, and make decisions under institutional incentives. This roundtable will discuss how multi-agent systems behave inside companies, markets, platforms, and governance environments — and what kinds of accountability, trust, incentive design, and institutional safety mechanisms may be needed.

This is an intimate, discussion-first gathering for people thinking seriously about agentic AI, AI safety, enterprise AI, governance, and the future of AI labor.

Learn more about Reign Dragon Lab: reigndragon.ai

This event is a part of #BOSTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.
properties.title: Governing the AI Workforce: Institutions, Incentives, and Accountability - #BOSTechWeek

properties.description:
As demand for energy security, resilient infrastructure, and robust supply chains rises, the boundaries between energy innovation and defense priorities are rapidly converging. This panel explores how government and private capital can be deployed to accelerate energy technologies by leveraging mission-driven demand, public procurement, and early-stage financing mechanisms.

Panelists will examine how federal agencies, including the Department of Energy and the Department of War, are supporting early traction through grants, SBIR/STTR programs, and direct procurement. The discussion will highlight the growing role of dual-use technologies, where defense adoption can provide early customers, validation, and initial deployment pathways for energy technology startups. The conversation will also explore how public funding and private investment can provide complementary signals that support technology validation and scale. Panelists will highlight practical models and lessons that can be applied across regions, agencies, and markets.

This session is designed for founders, investors, policymakers, and ecosystem builders looking to understand how national priorities are reshaping cap…

properties.description:
As part of the first ever Boston Tech Week, we’re bringing together people focused on getting real biology into the hands of consumers.

Boston has built its reputation on therapeutics and the FDA-driven path. This meetup is centered on a different model. Faster access, new distribution channels, and products that reach people directly without waiting on traditional systems, while still grounded in strong science and real outcomes.

There’s growing demand for health optimization, prevention, and performance. But most of the system is not designed to support it. That gap is where a lot of new building is happening.

This is not about generic wellness apps or passive tracking. It’s about:
- new ways of measuring biology that lead to action
- delivering efficacious biology in products that are safe and accessible
- creating paths to develop and access products without red tape

The format reflects that. This is a casual, conversation-first event held at Ginkgo Bioworks’ exclusive event space in the Seaport’s Drydock district.

We’ll start with a short welcome and intros from leaders actively building in the space including:
- Evan Zhao, CEO of Pacagen
- Rena Denoncourt, COO of Cors…

properties.description:
Most AI is theater.

Join us for a candid, behind-the-scenes conversation with leaders across product, sales. IT, venture, and academia on what’s actually happening inside AI adoption—and what’s just hype.

From real-world implementation challenges—like data quality, permissions, and model reliability—to broader questions around “AI-powered” products and autonomy, this session explores what it actually takes to make these systems work in practice.

Featured speakers include:
• Daniel Jaenicke, Director of B2B Product Strategy at MacPaw
• Maura Brady, Chief Sales Officer at a leading data and AI company
• Dr. Salem Othman, Associate Professor of Computer Science in the School of Computing and Data Science at Wentworth Institute of Technology

This event is a part of #BosTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.
properties.title: The emperor has no clothes: AI's dirty secret is that humans do all the work - #BosTechWeek

properties.description:
Right now, AI is a sports car.

In the hands of a small team that knows what it is doing, the right AI stack outships companies 10 times their size. The advantage is no longer who has the bigger team. It is who can drive faster without losing control.

The same stack that lets you outpace a competitor 10 times your size can also send the company off the road. Bad data. Hallucinated decisions. Customer trust shredded by an agent doing what an experienced operator would have caught.

For this Ugly Talk, we are bringing in Greg Cucino. He has been inside enough AI bets at growth-stage companies to know what wins and what wrecks them. He will walk you through what it takes to drive the sports car, and the mistakes that put founders in a wall.
properties.title:
Ugly Talk: How to Actually Build at the Speed of AI and Outship a Bigger Team
 #BOSTechWeek

properties.description:
Please join Boston University's Wheelock College of Education & Human Development and Just Horizons Alliance for the launch of the AI Ethics Index for K-12 Education. 

Hosted by Azer Bestavros, Associate Provost, Faculty of Computing & Data Sciences, this session brings together leaders from academia, civil society, and industry to explore a new tool for evaluating the use of artificial intelligence in K-12 education. The use of this tool and methodology will have relevance to every business sector. The event will feature a preview of the AI Ethics Index, a tool developed by Just Horizons Alliance, a non-profit applied research lab, founded by Wesley J. Wildman, who is also Boston University Duan Family Faculty Fellow and Professor of Computing & Data Sciences, and Professor of Philosophy, Theology, and Ethics. The AI Ethics Index is designed to help educators, policymakers, and companies evaluate the ethical dimensions of AI systems, particularly large language models used in educational settings. 

What makes this program distinctive is the partnership with BU Wheelock. Just Horizons will provide the integration of deep technical and ethical analysis with education expertise…

properties.description:
This is a two-day build sprint for engineering students who want to work on real startup problems instead of fake case prompts.

Before the event, we’ll collect 3 to 5 real technical issues from early-stage startups and open-source projects. Things like messy data pipelines, onboarding flows that don’t track correctly, internal tools that break when teams scale, or automation that no one has time to fix. Teams of 3 to 5 students will pick one real problem and build a working fix over 48 hours.

Every team must ship something concrete: a GitHub repo, short technical write-up, and live demo. The focus is working software, not slide decks. The event is alcohol-free and hosted on campus in Kendall Square.

Who is this event for? CS, data science, and engineering students in the Boston area, plus recent grads within three years who still want to build. This is for people who like debugging real systems, writing code, and shipping things that actually run. Attendees will be verified using .edu emails or graduation year.

This event is a part of #BOSTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

properties.description:
Every week, there’s a new headline about what AI is going to do to jobs, to schools, to the way we live. Most of those conversations are happening without kids in the room. AI Block Party puts them in the room.

It’s a half-day workshop-hackathon for students ages 11–14. Teams work with LEGO Education’s brand-new Computer Science & AI kits to tackle a real-world challenge - not a textbook exercise, not a tutorial. They build, they make decisions, they argue with each other about what the machine should do. Then they stand in front of a panel of tech founders and explain their thinking. That last part matters as much as the building.

We’re not trying to manufacture the next Zuckerberg. We’re trying to make sure that when AI shows up in these kids’ lives - in their schools, their future workplaces, the products they use every day; they understand it well enough to question it, shape it, and use it on their own terms. That’s what 
AI literacy actually means. It’s not a skill. It’s a disposition.

AI Block Party is built for the Tech Week community, because you’re the people who get this. If you’re a parent in tech who has thought about how to have a real conversation with your kid…

properties.description:
Mellea: Building Predictable, Auditable AI Systems

In this session we'll introduce Mellea, an open-source library that treats large language models, not as conversational interfaces, but as programmable components inside real applications. You'll see how Mellea is used to implement workflows that are predictable, auditable, and reproducible. Combined with smaller open-weight models like IBM's Granite models, developers can do Frontier Model Things but with better robustness and at a tiny fraction of the cost.

Bring a laptop. This session is open to engineers, AI builders, and PMs who are interested in leveraging the latest in open source LLMs and AI tools to build, not demos, but software that scales.
______

This is the last in a series of four "Build-n-Brew" sessions to be held in the afternoon of each day during Boston Tech Week, hosted at the MIT-IBM Computing Research Lab at "Pi on Main" (314 Main Street) in the heart of Kendall Square. Co-sponsored by The Open Accelerator, RedHat, and IBM, each session starts at 4pm and will be a hands-on "build" experience to learn and use the latest in open source AI tech, to be followed by a reception for a little nosh, "brew", and ne…

properties.description:
Hiring is broken, especially in a world where AI-generated resumes have made everyone look the same.

The Live Hiring Lab is a hands-on, founder-focused experiment exploring a simple question: what replaces the resume?

At this event, startup founders will bring real open roles, and we’ll evaluate real candidates in two ways, traditional resume-based screening vs AI-driven, adaptive interviews. Attendees will see how different signals emerge, compare outcomes in real time, and discuss what actually predicts strong hires.

This is not a panel or a pitch, it’s a live, interactive hiring experiment designed to surface what’s working, what’s broken, and what comes next.

If you’re hiring (or planning to soon), this will change how you think about evaluating talent.

If you are a job seeker, this is your chance to experience a new way of being evaluated, and see how your thinking, communication, and decision-making are assessed beyond a resume.

This event is a part of #BOSTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

A curated session for founders and investors focused on where to build and invest in AI driven education.

As AI rapidly reshapes education, we will examine specific opportunities where research is translating into scalable edtech products. The discussion will focus on evidence based learning systems, adaptive AI curricula, and clear pathways to measurable outcomes.

We will identify where capital can accelerate deployment, how to move faster from research to product, and what it takes to bring AI solutions into schools and institutions today.

The program includes a focused presentation on training AI agents for educators, with practical approaches to building, deploying, and integrating agent systems into real teaching workflows.

Bringing together founders, researchers, and investors, the goal is to align product, research, and capital to accelerate adoption and scale high impact edtech solutions.

Speakers include:

Ola Ozernov Palchik — MIT and BU, Evidence Based AI in Learning EVAL

properties.description:
Ship It Boston is a full-day build event during Boston Tech Week. You show up with an idea. You leave with something deployed. That's the whole format.

​Teams of 2 to 4 pick a problem in the morning and spend the day building a real product using Vercel V0, Cursor, Lovable, etc. Each candidate gets $100 coding credits and dedicated mentors. Not a scheduled drop-in. Someone who actually sits with you, works through blockers with you, and pushes you when you're stuck.

​At 4pm, every team pitches live. Five minutes to demo, five minutes of questions from a panel of VCs and investors. The Q&A opens to the whole room after pitches, so even teams that don't place walk away with real feedback from people who fund startups.

​Top three teams win gift cards, platform credits and sponsor swag.

​The day

​9:30am — Breakfast and check-in
10:00am — Build
12:30pm — Lunch
4:00pm — Pitches
5:30pm — Investor panel and open Q&A
6:00pm — Awards and reception

Engage: Participate in engaging discussions and explore topics critical to the intersection of AI, technology, and mental healthcare.

Learn: Discover cutting-edge solutions and emerging trends transforming how we approach mental health and wellness today.

Who Should Attend?
This event is open to everyone interested in Mental HealthTech, including:

- Entrepreneurs and startup founders
- Healthcare professionals
- Investors and industry experts
- Students and academics

Thank you to our sponsor Hult Business School ✨

RSVP Required: Space is limited, so be sure to secure your spot today.

This event is a part of #BosTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

properties.description:
The week is done. The events are over. The group chats are still buzzing.

Before everyone flies out, catches up on sleep, or disappears back into building mode—we're doing one last thing: coffee in Back Bay with the people who actually made #BOSTechWeek happen.

This is for the hosts who pulled off events on zero sleep. The sponsors who showed up and showed out. The founders and operators who were everywhere all week. And their friends who kept the energy going.

No agenda. No name tags. No one's going to ask about your raise. Just great coffee, good vibes, and the kind of unforced conversations that only happen when the pressure's off and everyone's riding the same high.

Think of it as the credits scene after the movie. The best connections happen here.

📍 Back Bay
🗓️ Sunday, May 31st · 8–11am ET
☕ Good coffee. Great people. Zero slide decks.

Show up. Decompress. Say the goodbyes you forgot to say.

properties.description:
Boston is known for many things, but sports and innovation stand out as two of its powerhouse industries. Now, those worlds are colliding—and the leaders driving that transformation are taking the stage at Fenway Park. Together, they’ll share how iconic franchises are using AI and modern data platforms to drive performance, deepen fan engagement, and unlock new business value—while offering a broader look at how these shifts are reshaping the global sports landscape.

Hear directly from:

- Josh Carley, VP of Technology, Boston Bruins
- Randy George, SVP of Technology, Boston Red Sox
- Randall Williams, Sports Business Reporter, Bloomberg

Veteran broadcast journalist Sarah Francomano will guide the discussion.

Part of #BOSTechWeek, this session offers an inside look at how leaders at the intersection of sports and innovation are building the future of the industry.
properties.title: The New MVP? Inside the AI Playbooks of the Red Sox and Bruins #BOSTechWeek

properties.description:
🌙 Tech Week Boston's closing night, hosted by Motif, with co-hosts MIT Sloan AI Club, and OpenArt. An intimate fireside to end the week on the questions most panels have been skipping.

What's actually missing from the future of sound?

The conversation around AI and music has been hijacked by one topic: generative music. But generation is just one layer of a much deeper set of unsolved, under-discussed, and commercially consequential problems.

Today's music AI models can't reliably produce music outside Western tonal systems — failing on the quarter tones, maqam, raga traditions, and more that underpin hundreds of millions of listeners' musical cultures. And we still don't adequately understand how people actually perceive and evaluate AI-generated vs. human-made music — a gap that shapes trust, adoption, and every creative tool built on top of these models.

An intimate fireside with two practitioners working on different frontiers of what music AI still gets wrong.

🎤 The panel

Jad Al Masri — Founder & CEO, Motif (Techstars '25). Violinist, Composer, Creative Director, Technologist, Lawyer, Writer. As a multicultural violinist and composer, and founder, he's building cultur…
properties.title: The Music AI Can't Hear: What's Actually Missing from the Future of Sound - #BOSTechWeek
